title: Glass Sphere & Refraction
blurb: One glass sphere, several ways: a refracting sphere in perfect focus, the same glass flattened into a lens, depth of field, and an animated morph from sphere to lens.

cards.title: Refracting Sphere
cards.desc: A glass sphere in a Cornell-style room, in perfect focus. It acts as a lens: the room appears inverted and shrunk inside it.
cards.meta: 1024x1024  -  3x3 anti-aliasing (9 rays/pixel)  -  up to 8 reflections/refractions  -  rendered in 4.3 s

cards.title: Flattened into a Lens
cards.desc: The same glass, flattened by an affine matrix into a thick disc facing the camera. Refraction through a transformed surface - a good stress test for the shader.
cards.meta: 1024x1024  -  3x3 anti-aliasing (9 rays/pixel)  -  up to 8 reflections/refractions  -  rendered in 8.4 s

cards.title: Depth of Field
cards.desc: The glass sphere shot through a thin lens focused mid-scene: the background boxes and walls blur while the spheres stay sharp.
cards.meta: 1024x1024  -  10x10 lens samples (depth of field)  -  up to 8 reflections/refractions  -  rendered in 49.0 s

cards.title: Sphere to Lens (animated)
cards.desc: Ramping the affine matrix from the round sphere (0.2*I) to the flatten transform in even steps. The lens tumbles through an edge-on view, revealing that it really is a thin disc rather than a sphere.
cards.meta: 480x480  -  16 frames, ping-pong loop  -  rendered in 10.5 s
